title: Foti Vito
content:
People.ContactEmail fvito@mccarthy.ca
Foti Vito is an associate in our Litigation Group in Toronto. He is developing a general litigation practice with a focus on commercial litigation, public law, and international disputes.
Foti first joined the firm as a summer student. Prior to returning as an associate, he clerked at the Court of Appeal for Ontario for Chief Justice Michael H. Tulloch, Justice Sarah E. Pepall, and Justice Patrick J. Monahan.
Foti received his Juris Doctor with Distinction from the University of Toronto Faculty of Law, where he was awarded the Ludwik & Estelle Jus Memorial Human Rights Prize. He also holds an Honours Bachelor of Arts with High Distinction in International Relations and Political Science from the University of Toronto, as well as a Certificate from The Hague Academy of International Law and a Diploma from the Siracusa International Institute. He was named an Arthur C. Helton Fellow by the American Society of International Law.
During law school, Foti was a Senior Editor of the University of Toronto Faculty of Law Review , a Rotman OnBoard Fellow, and a student volunteer with the International Human Rights Program, the Ontario Justice Education Network, and Downtown Legal Services. He completed an internship with the Office of the Prosecutor at the United Nations International Residual Mechanism for Criminal Tribunals in The Hague. Foti also represented Canada at the international rounds of the Philip C. Jessup International Law Moot Court Competition, where he received the Richard Baxter Award for best applicant memorial globally.
Foti serves as a Director of the Albanian Canadian Lawyers Association, where he helps guide the organization's strategic vision and leads its engagement initiatives. He is a member of the Law Society of Ontario.
